---
title: "Step 4: Organize State and Code Structure"
---

Once the simulation works, improve the shape of the code.

## Purpose

Use `struct` and better function boundaries to make the simulation easier to read, explain, and extend.

## Target Outcome

By the end of this step, you should have:

- a clearer representation of simulation state
- fewer loose variables
- functions with cleaner interfaces

## Prompt to Give the AI

```text
My Ising simulation now works, but the code feels scattered.
I want to organize the state better in Julia.

Please explain first:
- when it is useful to introduce a struct
- what information should belong in the simulation state
- how function signatures may improve after grouping related data

Then help me redesign the code in a minimal way.
Do not over-engineer it.
Keep the code readable for a beginner.
```

## Concepts to Acquire

- `struct`
- grouping related state
- simplifying function signatures
- separating data from operations

## A Minimal Version

```julia
struct IsingModel
    spins::Matrix{Int8}
    beta::Float64
end

function magnetization(model::IsingModel)
    return sum(model.spins) / length(model.spins)
end
```

This is not the only good design. The point is to make the role of each value clearer.

## Code-Reading Check

Read this:

```julia
struct IsingModel
    spins::Matrix{Int8}
    beta::Float64
end
```

Answer:

1. What information is grouped here?
2. Which function arguments might become unnecessary after this change?
3. Why might this be easier to explain to AI and to other humans?

## Extra Test Generation Prompt

```text
Create 3 short code-reading questions about using a struct to organize the state of a 2D Ising model in Julia.

Focus on:
- what information belongs in the struct
- how function signatures change
- why this improves code clarity

Do not give answers yet.
After I answer, grade my responses and give me 1 follow-up question.
```

## Skip Condition

You may skip this step if you can already:

- tell when a `struct` helps
- explain the difference between loose variables and grouped state
- redesign a few function signatures after introducing a state object
